hero.kicker: Recovery care funding for veterans and young people.
headline: Because recovery should not stop
when coverage does.

heroLead: 22STRONG helps pay for sports-medicine recovery care for veterans and young people when insurance no longer covers the next visit.

mission.heading: The next visit is often where recovery breaks down.
mission.lead: A care plan may be working, but the patient, family, or caregiver still has to get through approvals, scheduling, transportation, work or school conflicts, pain, paperwork, and the cost of repeated visits. When that next appointment is out of reach, progress can stall.
needParagraphs: Timing matters. Hands-on rehabilitative care is timing- and continuity-sensitive, and when visits are delayed, recovery can become harder to regain.
Recovery care works best when it stays on schedule. When cost gets in the way, we help keep the next visit within reach.

story.heading: The gap kept showing up in the treatment room.
paragraphs: 22STRONG started inside Next Level Fitness & Wellness Center, a sports-medicine clinic in Newport Beach co-owned by Travis Mayfield, a Purple Heart veteran of the 101st Airborne, and Dr. Vasili “Dr. Vas” Gatsinaris.

In the treatment room they kept seeing the same problem: the care was working, and the next visit wasn't guaranteed. They built 22STRONG so a recovery wouldn't depend on whether someone could afford one more appointment.

athletes.text: U.S. children were uninsured in 2024, the highest rate in nearly a decade, and many more are underinsured. Families are left to cover recovery care on their own.
athletes.source: Georgetown University Center for Children and Families

athletes.text: U.S. families had trouble paying their child's medical bills last year. When recovery care isn't covered, the cost falls on the family.
athletes.source: KFF / National Survey of Children's Health, 2024

athletes.text: Publicly insured pediatric orthopedic patients were more likely to face delays getting physical therapy after treatment.
of parents carry health care debt, far more than non-parents, and most of them have put off needed care because of the cost.

others.text: is the typical cost of one outpatient physical-therapy session for someone paying without insurance.
a year is a common insurance cap on physical therapy. Recovery often takes more.
lower risk of long-term opioid use when physical therapy starts early for musculoskeletal pain.

audiences.heading: Veterans
audiences.lead: Recovery care for veterans dealing with pain, injury, or limited mobility after service.
audiences.body: For veterans living with pain after service, waiting can mean more than a missed appointment. It can mean lost mobility, pain that flares back up, missed work, and the feeling of starting over. 22STRONG was built by people who have seen that gap up close, both through service and in the treatment room. That is why we help cover the next sports-medicine recovery visit when care is still needed and the appointment is out of reach.
audiences.photoAlt: Dr. Vas guiding a client through a strength exercise at Next Level Wellness

audiences.heading: Low-income youth
audiences.lead: Recovery care for young people whose families cannot cover the next visit.
audiences.body: For young people recovering from injury, surgery, illness, or ongoing pain, the next appointment can be where progress gets interrupted. Families may be dealing with insurance approvals, long waits, transportation, missed work, school schedules, pain, or the cost of repeated visits. 22STRONG helps cover recovery care when the next visit is needed and the family cannot carry it alone.
audiences.photoAlt: Youth basketball team huddling in a gym

featurePhoto.alt: Youth baseball players lined up along the dugout fence during a game

voices.name: Adam Folker
voices.quote: During my sophomore year in college, I had hip surgery that was potentially season-ending and even career-ending. My coaches referred me to Dr. Vas, who was able to take care of me and eventually get me back to playing within a short few weeks. That season I ended up winning hustle player of the year award.
voices.photoAlt: Adam Folker driving to the basket for UC Irvine

luke.story: Luke shares why access to hands-on recovery care matters after service.

kingMartin.story: King Martin shares his experience with 22STRONG Foundation's recovery care program.

veteranCare.story: A testimonial about veteran care and recovery support through 22STRONG Foundation.

adam.quote: During my sophomore year in college, I had hip surgery that was potentially season-ending and even career-ending. My coaches referred me to Dr. Vas, who was able to take care of me and eventually get me back to playing within a short few weeks. That season I ended up winning hustle player of the year award. Throughout my career, I saw Dr. Vas on various occasions for injuries that came up, along with maintenance and preventive care. I was able to play competitively at the top level.
adam.attribution: (more info on Adam's position, year, career)

veteranVoices.name: Tom Boscamp
veteranVoices.title: U.S. Army Veteran · Veteran Services Coordinator
veteranVoices.quote: I am a 65 year old Veteran who needs to be active since I am on the go taking care of Veterans in the Community. I have degenerative disk disease and Spinal Stenosis as well as radiculopathy. The VA treatments are far and few between as they do not have enough chiropractors, massage therapists, and Physical therapy specialists. Thankfully Dr. Vas has filled that void and with his treatments it has allowed me to continue being active. I have even lost 15 pounds! I can’t say enough how good Next Level Wellness is! Also their commitment to serving Veterans is “Next Level”!

strongCircle.heading: The 22STRONG Circle.
strongCircle.lead: A monthly membership that lets 22STRONG say yes to the next veteran or young person without waiting on the next fundraiser. Every nine members at $22 fund a recovery session, every month.
strongCircle.footnote: Monthly · cancel anytime · every dollar funds recovery care.

memberHighlight.text: A Green Beret whose Vietnam service is the subject of a Congressional Medal of Honor bill. Members like Ramon give monthly so the next veteran's visit is already funded.

points.label: Care that continues
points.text: Funding that keeps going after the first visit.

points.label: Money we can plan on
points.text: Monthly gifts let us commit to a full care plan up front.

heading: A recovery gap appears.
22STRONG closes it.
howItWorks.kicker: Someone has a care plan and the next appointment is in doubt. Here is what happens next.

steps.heading: Referral or request
steps.body: Referrals come from the VA, veteran networks, coaches, school counselors, and local community organizations.

visual.alt: Dr. Chris treating a veteran's leg during a recovery session
visual.label: A real injury becomes a request for help.

steps.heading: We confirm the gap
steps.body: 22STRONG reviews the situation. If coverage has run out or private care is out of reach, we step in.

steps.heading: Care funded
steps.body: 22STRONG pays the clinic directly. The patient never sees a bill.
steps.note: Care is currently provided through {provider} in Newport Beach, the sports-medicine clinic co-owned by 22STRONG's founders. Funded sessions can include Fascial Integrative Treatment (FIT) and hands-on care for rotator cuff, knee, neck, lower back, hip, ankle/foot, and wrist/elbow injuries.

disclosure.text: We publish how that provider relationship is managed.

leadership.name: Travis Mayfield
paragraphs: A Purple Heart recipient who served as a Sergeant with the U.S. Army’s 101st Airborne Division in Vietnam, Travis is Co-Owner and President of Next Level Fitness & Wellness Center. His own long road back from war is the inspiration behind 22STRONG’s model. He helped found the foundation and leads its mission to make recovery care accessible to those who served and to others rebuilding their lives.
His service and community work have been recognized by the California State Senate and Assembly, the U.S. House of Representatives, and Orange County.

leadership.name: Dr. Vasili Gatsinaris, D.C.
paragraphs: Dr. Vasili Gatsinaris, known to his patients as Dr. Vas, has been treating injuries for 25 years. He opened Next Level Wellness Center in Newport Beach in 2001 and still does the hands-on work himself. His patient list has included professional athletes from every major sports league, but most days the treatment room holds regular people. A veteran rebuilding a shoulder gets the same care an NBA player does.

He developed Fascial Integrative Treatment (FIT), the hands-on method used in many of the sessions 22STRONG funds. He earned his biology degree at UC Irvine and his Doctor of Chiropractic at the Los Angeles College of Chiropractic. He has been licensed in California since 2002.

governance.heading: The next layer belongs in public.
governance.lead: 22STRONG began with two founders and a working treatment room. Testimonials prove the care matters; governance has to prove the organization can outlast its founders. As board and advisory roles are formalized, that roster belongs here.

mission.label: Our mission
mission.statement: 22STRONG supports veterans and low-income youth by funding sports medicine recovery care when insurance no longer covers the next visit.

commitments.heading: Board and advisory roster
commitments.body: When board or advisory seats are formalized, 22STRONG will publish names, roles, and terms here so donors can see who shares oversight.

commitments.heading: Conflict standard
commitments.body: The Next Level relationship is named, linked, and governed by a disclosure rule: a conflicted leader should not approve a benefit to themselves or their business.

commitments.heading: Continuity path
commitments.body: The program is documenting referral review, funded-care records, and provider relationships so the model can keep operating as the organization grows.

donate.heading: Fund the next recovery appointment.
donate.lead: A one-time gift pays for hands-on care when the next visit is at risk.

storyImage.alt: Dr. Vas coaching a veteran through TRX suspension training
storyImage.caption: Recovery funding protects the long road from treatment to strength.

items.heading: Hands-on recovery sessions
items.body: Chiropractic, physical therapy, and massage therapy.

items.heading: Follow-through after the first visit
items.body: Funding that continues until the care plan is done.

items.heading: Provider and partner referrals
items.body: A way in for veterans and families who are already trying to get well.
